source.title: Task Demands Predict a Dynamic Switch in the Content of Awake Hippocampal Replay

confidence_rationale: Olafsdottir et al. demonstrate that awake hippocampal replay dynamically switches between task-focused (planning-mode) and consolidation-mode reactivation depending on task demands. Replay immediately before or after movement toward a reward preferentially encoded the current trajectory; during extended immobility, bias disappeared. Task-focused reactivations predicted subsequent decision accuracy. This is direct evidence that hippocampal replay is a context-sensitive, decision-relevant process -- supporting ARC-018's claim that the viability map is actively maintained and queried at decision time rather than being a static structure. The switch between modes (planning vs consolidation) also supports the hypothesis that the E3 harm/goal error signal gates replay content.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Awake hippocampal replay switches abruptly between task-focused reactivations (predicting navigational accuracy) near movement epochs and consolidation-mode reactivations during extended immobility; grid cells in entorhinal cortex engage coherently with hippocampus only during the consolidation mode.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-018 requires the viability map to be actively consulted at decision points and updated post-outcome. Olafsdottir et al. provide direct evidence for this dual-mode operation: the pre-movement replay corresponds to map consultation (rollout generation); post-movement replay at the reward site corresponds to E3 harm/goal error-driven update of the map. The dynamic switch is consistent with the hypothesis that the same hippocampal structure serves both rollout generation and post-outcome viability updating.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The indexing mechanism is spatial -- place cells in CA1. ARC-018 V3 requires action-object coordinates from E2. The paper does not address whether the same switch mechanism operates in non-spatial domains. The entorhinal grid cell coherence finding (consolidation mode only) is not directly predicted by ARC-018 and may reflect additional mechanisms.
mapping.source_context: Rodent (male rat), self-paced spatial task with reward locations, simultaneous CA1 + medial entorhinal cortex recording.

confidence_components.notes: The dual-mode finding is important for ARC-018 -- it supports the claim that the same hippocampal structure handles both rollout and post-outcome update. The spatial-only context limits transfer confidence for the action-object framing.
